action.reasoning: Observation: The training is producing NaN losses after a few epochs, which indicates numerical instability in my Generalized Cross Entropy (GCE) implementation. The accuracy is also very low (1.14%). This is likely due to the q parameter in GCE being too small (0.7) causing numerical issues when raising probabilities to the power of q, especially when probabilities are close to 0. Analysis: The GCE formula (1 - p^q) / q can become unstable when p is very small and q is also small. I need to add numerical stability to prevent this. Also, I should try a higher q value. Decision: I'll modify the GCE implementation to add epsilon for numerical stability and try a higher q value (e.g., 0.9).
